title: lasiorrhiza cerberoana
intro: Lasiorrhiza cerberoana, also known as the Cerbera plant, is a type of toxic plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. Its medicinal properties are derived from the seeds, which contain a toxic compound called cerberin, but also have potential health benefits when used properly. The seeds of the Lasiorrhiza cerberoana plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by extracting the seeds' oil or using them in tinctures, but it is crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using this plant due to its toxicity.
